source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A DC series motor fed from rated supply voltage is overloaded and its magnetic circuit is saturated. The torque-speed characteristic of this motor will be approximately represented by which curve of below figure?
extraction_notes: image field is present as an in-memory PIL image representation, but no image path or URL is provided
raw_answer_text mapped from solution letter B to choice text

image_dependency: question_text explicitly asks which curve in the below figure represents the characteristic
answer options are figure-referenced labels (Curve A/B/C/D), so the visual is needed to map the concept to an option
asset_registry_record shows an image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: requires applying motor-domain knowledge about DC series motor torque-speed behavior under overload and magnetic saturation
then requires matching the inferred behavior to one of the plotted curves in the figure
multiple-choice selection among 4 curve labels indicates concept-to-visual mapping rather than direct text lookup
verifiability: answer is a short, discrete choice-style response: 'Curve B'
choices are explicitly enumerated as A/B/C/D, making final-answer checking straightforward
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support reliable downstream checking
because correctness depends on figure interpretation and domain knowledge, verifiability is high but not maximal

decision_reasons: question text, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantic assets are intact
asset registry passed with no reported issues
image dependency is very high and the required figure is available
verifiability is high because the answer is a short discrete curve label
multi-step reasoning demand is substantial, which supports candidate value despite domain knowledge being required
